metadata.title: Triton AI Knowledge Map — Self-Help & Self-Service Resources
metadata.description: Structured knowledge base for RAG supporting the AI intake redesign project at UC San Diego. Covers all self-help, self-service, training, and tool resources available through Triton AI and the broader UCSD AI ecosystem.
metadata.created_by: Zach Johnson, Director of AI Strategy, UC San Diego
metadata.purpose: AI intake redesign — RAG knowledge base

primary_hub.name: Triton AI
primary_hub.description: Comprehensive online hub for the UC San Diego campus community to discover, access, and learn about all available AI services and resources.
primary_hub.scope: UC San Diego Campus and Health Sciences

related_ucsd_sites.title: UC San Diego's Support of AI Services (Blink)
Using Commercial GenAI Tools in Administration
Guidelines for Uses of AI Assistants (Privacy Office)

tools.name: TritonGPT
tools.description: UC San Diego's secure, on-premise generative AI platform hosted at the San Diego Supercomputer Center. Provides AI-powered support for campus policies, content generation, document summarization, and more.
tools.hosting: On-premise at San Diego Supercomputer Center with additional cloud model options
tools.scope: UC San Diego Campus and Health Sciences
tools.access_method: SSO login at tritongpt.ucsd.edu; eligible users receive an email with login link
tools.data_security: University maintains full control over data; local models keep data on the UC San Diego network; cloud models are clearly labeled
capabilities: Ask UC San Diego-related questions about campus policies, procedures, and services
AI-assisted writing: presentation outlines, emails, draft communications, summaries
Document summarization: paste policies, forms, or long text
Content editing: refine drafts, improve tone and grammar
Job description drafting (Job Description Helper assistant)

available_models.name: GPT OSS
available_models.notes: On-premises; data never leaves UC San Diego network

available_models.name: Llama 4 Scout
available_models.notes: On-premises; data never leaves UC San Diego network

assistants.name: UC San Diego Assistant
assistants.purpose: Provides information about UCSD policies, procedures, and campus life
assistants.data_sources: UCSD Blink pages and other internal documentation

assistants.name: General AI Assistant
assistants.purpose: Supports writing, editing, and content generation without UCSD-specific context

assistants.name: Internet Search
assistants.purpose: Provides information retrieval from the web

assistants.name: Fund Manager Coach
assistants.purpose: Guidance on budgeting, grants, financial transactions, payroll, and compliance
assistants.data_sources: Finance and Research Knowledge Base Articles from Services & Support, Blink pages on finance, research, procurement, travel & expense

assistants.name: Job Description Helper
assistants.purpose: Assists with writing Position Overviews for Career Tracks library jobs

assistants.name: Expert Notetaker
assistants.purpose: Converts raw meeting inputs into clear, structured documentation
features: Accepts Zoom/Teams transcripts or rough notes

steps: Eligible users receive email with login link
Do not attempt access until confirmation email arrives
Choose the right assistant for your task
Subscribe to TritonAI mailing list for updates

tools.name: TritonGPT Chatbot Widget
tools.description: Embeddable AI-powered chatbot widget for UC San Diego department websites. Provides natural-language support and information retrieval directly on webpages without requiring user authentication.
tools.cost: Free (potential cost savings vs. third-party chatbot services)
capabilities: Lightweight embed on any UC San Diego CMS website
Natural language understanding grounded in institutional knowledge
No authentication required for end users
Draws from curated knowledge base of 60+ UC San Diego websites

self_service_paths.action: Explore the widget for your department
steps: Reach out to explore whether it can replace third-party chatbot services

tools.name: Copilot with Data Protection
tools.description: Microsoft Copilot chatbot with data protection features. Provides image generation, PDF summarization, content generation, and fact retrieval.

tools.name: Copilot for Microsoft 365
tools.description: AI-powered productivity tool integrated with Word, Excel, PowerPoint, Outlook, Teams, and other Microsoft 365 apps. Assists with drafting documents, summarizing emails, creating presentations, analyzing data, and generating insights.
tools.cost: Recharge (department purchase required); currently in soft launch
eligibility: Departments enrolled in UC San Diego Campus and School Agreement (CASA)

tools.name: Zoom AI Companion
tools.description: AI features within UC San Diego's Zoom instance. Disabled by default; users opt in through their individual Zoom account settings.
tools.cost: Free (included with UC San Diego Zoom)

steps: Log into your UC San Diego Zoom account

tools.name: Google Gemini
tools.description: Google's multimodal AI model powering Google's AI chatbot. Available to all UC San Diego campus users.
tools.scope: UC San Diego campus (Health Sciences staff can request access via exception process)

tools.name: NotebookLM
tools.description: Google's AI-powered research and note-taking tool for document interaction. Leverages Gemini to summarize content, answer questions with citations, generate ideas, and create audio overviews.
tools.scope: UC San Diego campus (Health Sciences staff can request access via exception process)
capabilities: Question answering grounded in uploaded documents

tools.name: GitHub Copilot
tools.description: AI-powered coding assistant for developers. Reported to boost developer productivity at UC San Diego.

training_and_learning.name: AI Foundations (Self-Paced)
training_and_learning.description: Foundational introduction to AI terminology and history. Recommended starting point for users beginning their AI journey.
training_and_learning.url_context: Available through UCLC; referenced on tritonai.ucsd.edu training pages

training_and_learning.name: TritonGPT Interface Guide
training_and_learning.description: Step-by-step guide for first-time TritonGPT users to understand the platform interface and familiarize themselves with available assistants.

training_and_learning.name: Prompting Best Practices Guide
training_and_learning.description: Guide on crafting effective prompts for TritonGPT and other AI tools.
key_principles: Be specific — more detail yields better responses
Explore use cases — brainstorming, summarizing, drafting
Verify sources — review links provided in responses
Provide feedback — use thumbs-up/down and comments

training_and_learning.name: AI Webinars & Workshops
training_and_learning.description: Hands-on prompt writing workshops available both online and in person. Focus on getting the best outputs from AI tools, with skills applicable to TritonGPT, Gemini, Copilot, ChatGPT, and others.

example_sessions.location: Green Table Room, Price Center West

training_and_learning.name: TritonGPT: Unlocking the Power of AI (Webinar Slides)
training_and_learning.description: PDF presentation from an introductory TritonGPT webinar covering platform overview and capabilities.

programs.name: Instructional AI Pilot
programs.description: Pilot program exploring how secure, generative AI can enhance teaching and learning. Powered by TritonGPT, it provides custom AI assistants for participating instructors.
programs.how_it_works: Grounds AI knowledge in course materials (lecture slides, study guides, readings, transcripts). Instructors manage content via secure Google Drive integration.

assistant_types.name: Student-facing assistant
assistant_types.purpose: Socratic engagement — guides students through dialogue rather than providing direct answers

programs.content_management: Instructors create a Google Drive folder named with their course ID; subfolders auto-created for each assistant type

timeline.july_sept: Course content upload and assistant configuration
timeline.aug_dec: Both assistants live for Fall quarter

policies_and_governance.name: Using Commercial GenAI Tools in Administration
policies_and_governance.description: Guidelines for how campus administrators should evaluate and use commercial generative AI tools.
key_points: Adhere to all laws, regulations, UC policies, and UC Principles of Community
Follow security standards and vendor terms of use
Never share PII, FERPA-protected student records, or P3/P4 classified data without proper contractual safeguards

policies_and_governance.name: Guidelines for Uses of AI Assistants (Privacy Office)
policies_and_governance.description: Privacy-focused guidelines from the UC San Diego Privacy Office on responsible AI assistant use.

policies_and_governance.name: TritonGPT Privacy Statement
policies_and_governance.description: Privacy statement specific to the TritonGPT platform.

policies_and_governance.name: Data Classification and AI Use
policies_and_governance.source: Synthesized from multiple UCSD policy pages
key_rules: Never share PII with AI services without proper safeguards
Never share P3 or P4 classified data without contractual protections
Consult UCOP website for data classification guidance if uncertain
Local TritonGPT models keep data on the UC San Diego network
Cloud models are clearly labeled in the TritonGPT interface

policies_and_governance.name: Artificial Intelligence in Education — Academic Integrity
policies_and_governance.description: Academic integrity guidance on the use of generative AI in educational contexts.

policies_and_governance.name: AI Guidelines for Campus Communicators
policies_and_governance.description: Guidelines for campus communications professionals using AI tools.

policies_and_governance.name: AI Development Workgroup
policies_and_governance.description: Governance body for AI tool development at UC San Diego.
policies_and_governance.role: Guides development and evolution of campus AI services

policies_and_governance.name: UC Responsible AI Principles
policies_and_governance.description: University of California system-wide recommendations for responsible AI use, adopted across all UC campuses.

support_channels.for: TritonGPT access issues, general AI tool support

support_channels.description: Subscribe for latest announcements and news about AI services
Thumbs-up/thumbs-down plus comment fields within TritonGPT

self_service_journeys.description: Common user paths through the self-service ecosystem, organized by persona and intent. Designed for intake routing and deflection.

journeys.persona: New campus user wanting to try AI

steps.action: Visit tritonai.ucsd.edu to discover available tools
Complete AI Foundations self-paced course in UCLC
Wait for TritonGPT access confirmation email
Review TritonGPT Interface Guide
Log in at tritongpt.ucsd.edu and explore assistants
Review Prompting Best Practices guide

journeys.persona: Staff member needing campus policy info
journeys.intent: Find answers about UC San Diego policies or procedures

steps.action: Open TritonGPT at tritongpt.ucsd.edu
Select the UC San Diego Assistant
Ask your policy or procedure question
Review source links provided in the response
Provide feedback via thumbs-up/down

journeys.persona: Staff or faculty needing to draft content
journeys.intent: Generate or refine written content (emails, presentations, summaries)

steps.action: Open TritonGPT
Select the General AI Assistant
Provide a detailed, specific prompt
Iterate on the output
Review Prompting Best Practices if needed

journeys.intent: Get guidance on budgets, grants, financial transactions, compliance

steps.action: Open TritonGPT
Select the Fund Manager Coach assistant
Ask your finance, procurement, or compliance question
Review source documentation referenced in the response

journeys.persona: Anyone who needs to document a meeting
journeys.intent: Turn meeting transcripts into structured notes

steps.action: Get transcript from Zoom AI Companion or Teams
Open TritonGPT and select Expert Notetaker
Paste or upload the transcript
Receive structured notes focused on decisions and action items

journeys.intent: Add AI-powered support to a department website

steps.action: Review TritonGPT Chatbot Widget info page
Evaluate whether the widget can replace existing third-party chatbot
Contact the TritonGPT team to explore implementation
Embed widget on UC San Diego CMS website

journeys.persona: Faculty member wanting AI in their course
journeys.intent: Set up course-specific AI assistants for teaching

steps.action: Review Instructional AI Pilot information
Apply to participate in the pilot program
Create Google Drive folder with course ID
Upload course materials to appropriate subfolders
Configure instructor-facing and student-facing assistants
Deploy assistants for the quarter

journeys.persona: Any user unsure about what data to share with AI
journeys.intent: Understand data classification rules for AI tools

steps.action: Review acceptable use policy on Blink
Check data classification (P1-P4) for your data
If P3/P4, use only local TritonGPT models (data stays on campus)
If uncertain, consult UCOP data classification guidance or contact Privacy Office

resources: Using Commercial GenAI Tools in Administration
Guidelines for Uses of AI Assistants

journeys.persona: Campus user wanting to improve AI skills
journeys.intent: Attend training and build prompting skills

steps.action: Complete AI Foundations self-paced course in UCLC
Review Prompting Best Practices guide
Register for upcoming AI webinar/workshop via UCLC
Attend live session (online or in-person)
Practice with TritonGPT using techniques learned
Subscribe to TritonAI mailing list for ongoing updates

rag_tags.description: Flat list of tags for chunk-level metadata to improve retrieval quality
